name: Newar
notes: Newar (Nepal Bhasa, नेपाल भाषा) of the Kathmandu Valley, written today in Devanagari (historically in the Ranjana and other Nepalese scripts). The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified: Devanagari is an abugida with an inherent vowel, dependent vowel signs, conjuncts and the virama, and the Newar sound system (including its tonal/phonation and vowel-length contrasts) does not map to the Devanagari letters the same way Nepali or Hindi does. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode newa1246), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode newa1246.

ancestors.notes: Sino-Tibetan — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Kathmandu Valley Newari.
